LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. COMMERCIAL CODE SECTION 3307(A)(1) GOVERNS. MCCARTHY DEPOSITED THE CHECKS AS A GENERAL PARTNER AND THE BANK HAD KNOWLEDGE OF THAT FACT AND THAT MCCARTHY WAS DEPOSITING THE CHECK IN HIS ACCOUNT. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER MCCARTHY WAS AN AGENT OF PLAINTIFF UNDER CORPORATIONS CODE SECTION 16301. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT ON THE AIDING AND ABETTING CAUSE OF ACTION. Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Audit File
Off Calendar Apr-18-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Gail S. Greenwood, Esq., for for plaintiff and moving party: Quail Creek Vineyards
Christopher J. Borders, Esq., for defendant and opposing party: Wells Fargo Bank, N. A.

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Quail Creek Vineyards' motion to compel production of documents pursuant to subpoena is denied. The Court finds that the audit conducted by Nigro Karlin at the request of counsel for defendant is qualified work product within the meaning of CCP Section 2018.030(b). The Court does not find that denial of disclosure will unfairly prejudice plaintiff or result in an injustice. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-08-2007 Denied
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: GAIL S. GREENWOOD, ESQUIRE (FRIEDMAN, DUMAS & SPRINGWATER, 415.834.3800) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. PAUL VALLONE, ESQUIRE (HINSHAW & CULBERTSON, 415.263.8104) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. COMMERCIAL CODE SECTION 3307(A)(1) GOVERNS. McCARTHY DEPOSITED THE CHECKS AS A GENERAL PARTNER AND THE BANK HAD KNOWLEDGE OF THAT FACT AND THAT McCARTHY WAS DEPOSITING THE CHECK IN HIS ACCOUNT. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER McCARTHY WAS AN AGENT OF PLAINTIFF UNDER CORPORATIONS CODE SECTION 16301. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT ON THE AIDING AND ABETTING CAUSE OF ACTION. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses By Wells Fargo Bank To Special Interrogatories And Document Requests; And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-30-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
(Telephonic Appearance) Gail Greenwood for plaintiff and moving party: Quail Creek Vineyards
Paul E. Vallone for defendant and opposing party: Wells Fargo Bank

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted with modification: Plaintiff' Quail Creek vineyards' motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories 1, 121-128 (set #1) and request for production of documents 28, 30-33, 40 (set #2) is granted. As to the request for production, defendant is ordered to produce responsive documents from January 1, 2000 to the present upon the parties entering into a protective order. The order shall provide that any documents may only be used for purposes related to the litigation and may not be disseminated. However, counsel for plaintiff may disclose documents to persons employed by counsel to assist in the preparation of the case, if that disclosure is required for that preparation. Persons provided this information shall be informed that further dissemination of the information constitutes a violation of a court order.
MODIFICATION:That production of documents limited to wholesale or business account information. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.
